content.problemStatement:
For a function fff, define f1(x)=f(x)f^1(x) = f(x)f1(x)=f(x) and fm(x)=f(fm−1(x))f^m(x) = f(f^{m - 1}(x))fm(x)=f(fm−1(x)) for all m>1m > 1m>1.

You are given 222 integers NNN and KKK.

Find the number of functions f:{1,2,3,…,N+K}→{1,2,3,…,N+K}f : \{1, 2, 3, \ldots, N + K\} \rightarrow \{1, 2, 3, \ldots, N + K\}f:{1,2,3,…,N+K}→{1,2,3,…,N+K} satisfying the following conditions:


f(x)=xf(x) = xf(x)=x for all x>Nx > Nx>N.
fN(x)>Nf^N(x) > NfN(x)>N for all xxx.
fN(x)≤fN(x+1)f^N(x) \le f^N(x + 1)fN(x)≤fN(x+1) for all 1≤x<N1 \le x < N1≤x<N.
Since the answer may be large, print it modulo 998244353998244353998244353.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first and only line of input contains 222 integers - NNN and KKK.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the number of functions modulo 998244353998244353998244353.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10510^5105.
